modules.title: Treaty Terminal
modules.tagline: Treaty rights, consultation depth, cumulative-effects doctrine, and the post-Yahey precedent
modules.lede: The Treaty Terminal tracks legal and governance signals across major Canadian infrastructure decisions: where treaty rights and Aboriginal title interact with project approvals, where Yahey-style cumulative-effects findings reshape the rules, and where consultation depth meets the limits of Alberta's narrowing 'directly and adversely affected' standard.

modules.title: Water Intelligence
modules.tagline: Watershed allocation, drought signals, mine-water climate stress, and First Nation water rights
modules.lede: Water Intelligence tracks where projects are being approved against an over-allocated southern basin system (St. Mary > 100% of median flow), against Aboriginal Extreme Flow trends on the Lower Athabasca, and against a doctrinal baseline (Tsuu T'ina v. Alberta, 2008) that found no standalone Treaty 6/7 right to water.

modules.title: Energy & Grid Intelligence
modules.tagline: AESO large-load queue, Phase I/II framework, NERC reliability findings, and Treaty-territory grid stress
modules.lede: Energy & Grid Intelligence tracks the largest gap in Canadian-grid planning right now: > 20 GW of data-centre load applications against a 1,200 MW Phase I cap, a record 12,785 MW winter peak, transmission costs spiking 300%+ year-over-year, and concentration in Treaty 8 / Treaty 6 corridors where Indigenous consultation has not yet been documented at project scale.

modules.title: Indigenous Finance
modules.tagline: CIB / AIOC / FNFA / CILGC architecture, Coastal GasLink forfeiture precedent, and the Hydro One model
modules.lede: Indigenous Finance tracks the layered architecture of Canadian Indigenous-equity capital: CIB Indigenous Equity Initiative ($5–$100M loans up to 90% LTV), AIOC ($1B→$3B Alberta envelope, $20M floor), federal CILGC ILGP ($20M–$1B per deal with 75% federal stacking cap), and FNFA's $3.9B portfolio at AA-/Aa3/AA(low). The Coastal GasLink forfeiture (FNMPC, 2024) is the canonical documented case for why this architecture matters; the Hydro One 50%-equity model is the active comparator precedent.
